description: Security Review & Hardening — fixes vulnerabilities and adds defense-in-depth for public internet deployment (Infomaniak VPS). No breaking API changes.

userStories.title: Constant-time API key comparison
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want the API key comparison to be timing-safe so that an attacker cannot recover the key by measuring response times.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/security.py: import `secrets` from stdlib
Replace `api_key != settings.api_key` with `not secrets.compare_digest(api_key or "", settings.api_key or "")` in verify_api_key()
If settings.api_key is empty string (no auth configured), behavior is unchanged: all requests pass
If settings.api_key is set, wrong key returns HTTP 401, correct key returns HTTP 200
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Rate limiting on /v1/health endpoint
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the health endpoint to be rate-limited so it cannot be used for reconnaissance or amplification attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/api/v1/endpoints/health.py: add `@limiter.limit(f"{settings.rate_limit_per_minute}/minute")` decorator on the health() function
Import `limiter` from `app.security` and `settings` from `app.config`
Import `Request` from `fastapi` and add `request: Request` as first parameter to health()
GET /v1/health still returns HTTP 200 with {"status": "ok", ...} for normal requests
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: limiter.limit decorator aggiunto su health(); request: Request come primo param

userStories.title: Toggle /docs and /redoc via DOCS_ENABLED env var
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to disable Swagger UI and ReDoc in production via an environment variable so the API schema is not publicly visible.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/config.py: add `docs_enabled: bool = True` to Settings class
In app/main.py: pass `docs_url="/docs" if settings.docs_enabled else None` and `redoc_url="/redoc" if settings.docs_enabled else None` to FastAPI() constructor
Default `docs_enabled=True` preserves current behavior for local development
In .env.example: add `DOCS_ENABLED=false  # false in produzione` line
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: docs_enabled: bool = True in config.py; docs_url/redoc_url condizionali in main.py; DOCS_ENABLED=false in .env.example

userStories.title: Add CSP, HSTS, and Permissions-Policy security headers
userStories.description: As a user, I want the browser to receive Content-Security-Policy, HSTS, and Permissions-Policy headers so the frontend is protected against injection and downgrade attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/security.py add_security_headers(): add `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; style-src 'self' https://fonts.googleapis.com; font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com; script-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'`
Add `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains`
Add `Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), camera=(), microphone=()`
Add `X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies: none`
All previously existing headers (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, Cache-Control) are preserved unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: CSP, HSTS, Permissions-Policy, X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies aggiunti in add_security_headers() in app/security.py

userStories.title: Proxy-aware rate limiting via TRUSTED_PROXIES
userStories.description: As an operator behind Nginx, I want the rate limiter to read the real client IP from X-Forwarded-For so that all users are not counted as the same proxy IP.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/config.py: add `trusted_proxies: str = ""` to Settings (comma-separated IPs, empty = direct connection)

In app/security.py: add function `get_real_ip(request: Request) -> str` — if request.client.host is in the configured trusted_proxies list AND X-Forwarded-For header is present, return first IP from X-Forwarded-For; otherwise return get_remote_address(request)
Replace `key_func=get_remote_address` with `key_func=get_real_ip` in the Limiter() instantiation
In .env.example: add `TRUSTED_PROXIES=  # IP del reverse proxy, es: 127.0.0.1` line
Existing rate limiting behavior is unchanged when TRUSTED_PROXIES is empty
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: get_real_ip() aggiunta in app/security.py; trusted_proxies: str = '' in config.py; TRUSTED_PROXIES in .env.example; limiter usa get_real_ip come key_func

userStories.title: Remove filesystem info disclosure from YAML loader error
userStories.description: As an operator, I want error messages to not reveal which fiscal year files exist on the server so that attackers cannot enumerate the filesystem.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/rules/loader.py: remove the `available` variable and the `f'Disponibili: ...'` clause from the FileNotFoundError message
New error message is only: `f'Anno fiscale {fiscal_year} non supportato.'`
The HTTP status code returned by the endpoint (422 for calculate, 404 for rules) is unchanged
POST /v1/deduction/calculate with fiscal_year=1900 returns 422; response body does NOT contain the word 'Disponibili'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Rimossa variabile 'available' e clausola 'Disponibili:' da loader.py; messaggio ora solo 'Anno fiscale {fiscal_year} non supportato.'

userStories.title: Input length and format validation on Address fields
userStories.description: As an operator, I want address fields to have length limits so that excessively long strings are rejected before being forwarded to external geocoding APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/schemas/request.py Address model: city gets `Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=100)`
street gets `Field(default=None, max_length=200)`
postal_code gets `Field(..., min_length=3, max_length=10)` (no regex, to stay compatible with both CH NPA 4-digit and IT CAP 5-digit formats)
country gets `Field(default='CH', min_length=2, max_length=2)` so only 2-char codes are accepted
POST with city containing 200 characters returns HTTP 422
POST with country='SVIZZERA' (7 chars) returns HTTP 422
All existing test fixtures (Lugano 6900, Bellinzona 6500, etc.) continue to return HTTP 200
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Field constraints aggiunti su Address: city max_length=100, street max_length=200, postal_code min_length=3 max_length=10, country min_length=2 max_length=2

userStories.title: Make Nominatim contact email configurable
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the personal email in the Nominatim User-Agent to be set via environment variable so it is not hardcoded in the public source code.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/config.py: add `nominatim_contact_email: str = "admin@example.com"` to Settings
In app/geo/providers/nominatim.py: remove the hardcoded email from `_HEADERS`; build User-Agent header dynamically at call time reading `settings.nominatim_contact_email`
Import `settings` from `app.config` in nominatim.py
In .env.example: add `NOMINATIM_CONTACT_EMAIL=tua@email.com  # per User-Agent Nominatim` line
Geocoding still works: User-Agent header is still present, just with configurable email
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Run Docker container as non-root user
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the Docker container to run as a non-privileged user so that a container escape or RCE does not give root access to the host.
acceptanceCriteria: In Dockerfile: add `RUN addgroup --system appgroup && adduser --system --ingroup appgroup --no-create-home appuser` before CMD
Add `USER appuser` immediately after the adduser line
The existing Dockerfile structure (COPY, uv setup, site-packages workaround, EXPOSE 8000, CMD) is preserved
`docker build -t ddc-test .` from D:\006-Documenti\Lavori\DdC\002_Applicativo completes without errors
`docker run --rm -p 8099:8000 ddc-test` → GET http://localhost:8099/v1/health returns HTTP 200
Typecheck passes (no Python changes)

userStories.title: Add pip-audit for dependency vulnerability scanning
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a documented command to audit dependencies for known CVEs so that vulnerabilities are caught before deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: In pyproject.toml [dependency-groups] dev: add `"pip-audit>=2.7"` entry

Run `uv run pip-audit` from D:\006-Documenti\Lavori\DdC\002_Applicativo; if vulnerabilities are found, update the affected packages to fixed versions in pyproject.toml and regenerate uv.lock with `uv lock`
Create docs/security.md with: `# Security` heading, `## Dependency audit` section, command `uv run pip-audit`, one-sentence description
`uv run pip-audit` exits with code 0 (zero known vulnerabilities)
All existing 69+ tests still pass with updated dependencies
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix ALLOWED_ORIGINS wildcard warning in .env.example
userStories.description: As an operator following the deployment guide, I want .env.example to warn against ALLOWED_ORIGINS=* so I do not accidentally expose the API to all origins in production.
acceptanceCriteria: In .env.example: change the ALLOWED_ORIGINS value from `*` to `http://localhost:8000`
Add comment line above: `# CORS — NON usare * in produzione! Esempio: https://tuo-dominio.ch`
If docs/deploy-infomaniak-nano.md exists: add a warning paragraph near the .env configuration step explaining that ALLOWED_ORIGINS=* exposes the API to cross-origin requests from any website
If docs/deploy-infomaniak-nano.md does not exist: only update .env.example
No Python code changes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Security test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated tests for all critical security behaviors so that regressions are caught immediately in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/unit/test_security.py with tests: test_api_key_empty_allows_all (settings.api_key='' → verify_api_key does not raise), test_api_key_wrong_raises_401 (wrong key → HTTPException 401), test_api_key_correct_passes (correct key → no exception), test_get_real_ip_direct (no trusted proxies → client.host returned), test_get_real_ip_from_xfwd (client.host in trusted list + X-Forwarded-For header → forwarded IP returned), test_postal_code_too_long_invalid (postal_code 20 chars → ValidationError), test_country_too_long_invalid (country='CHE' → ValidationError)

Create tests/integration/test_security_headers.py with tests: test_security_headers_present (GET / → response has Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Permissions-Policy), test_health_endpoint_responds (GET /v1/health → status 200)
All new tests pass: `uv run pytest tests/unit/test_security.py tests/integration/test_security_headers.py -v`
All existing 69+ tests still pass: `uv run pytest tests/ -v`
Typecheck passes
